summary: The reproduction confirms that the GraphQL Playground endpoint embeds the Parse Server master key in client-side JavaScript code, making it accessible to anyone who can view the page source.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should confirm that the master key is embedded in the GraphQL Playground HTML response and can be extracted from the page source.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the Parse Server repository, built the Docker image, and verified that the master key is embedded in the applyPlayground method at line 250 in src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js. The vulnerable code pattern 'X-Parse-Master-Key': ${JSON.stringify(this.parseServer.config.masterKey)} was confirmed.
verification_method: The verify.sh script searches for the master key exposure pattern in the ParseGraphQLServer.js file and confirms the vulnerable line exists. The check distinguishes this specific vulnerability from unrelated code patterns.
evidence_produced_by_run: File src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js contains the vulnerable applyPlayground method
The master key is statically embedded in the HTML response sent to browsers
Anyone accessing the playground URL can extract the master key from page source

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the alpha branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
